full_content:
Specification capture transparency: surface every capture event + present full text on approve/reject

Prime Builder MUST surface every specification-capture decision to the owner in chat in real time. This applies to BOTH outcomes:

POSITIVE PATH (capture):
- When the AI classifies an owner statement (or a Loyal Opposition advisory report, or any inbound text) as a candidate for: a requirement, a specification (SPEC/REQ), a governance rule (GOV), a protected behavior (PB), an architecture decision (ADR), a design constraint (DCL), or a deliberation (DELIB) — the AI must surface that classification to the owner in chat at the moment of classification, not batched at session-end.
- The surfacing must include: what was classified, why it was classified that way, what artifact would be created, and what action the AI is requesting (approve / acknowledge / nothing-required).

NEGATIVE PATH (decline-to-capture):
- When the AI considers an owner statement (or other inbound text) for classification and decides NOT to capture it as any artifact, the AI must surface that decline-decision to the owner in chat at the moment of the decision.
- The surfacing must include: what was considered, why the AI judged it non-capture-worthy (e.g., "conversational, not prescriptive"; "duplicates DELIB-XXXX"; "below capture threshold per DCL-ARTIFACT-LIFECYCLE-TRIGGERS-001"), and an explicit invitation for the owner to override the decision.

APPROVE/REJECT WITH FULL TEXT:
- When the formal-artifact-approval gate fires for any formal artifact (GOV, SPEC, PB, ADR, DCL, DELIB, or document-category artifacts like IPR/CVR/implementation_proposal/constraint_verification) — the owner MUST see the FULL proposed artifact text in the approval prompt: not a summary, not a paraphrase, not a link to a file, not a hash.
- The owner's approve / reject / edit-and-approve decision MUST itself be captured as a sub-DELIB or as the existing approval packet's approved_by/acknowledged_by + transcript_captured fields.

Rationale:
The Slice 8.6 incident (S330, 2026-05-04) revealed that the AI's silent decline-to-capture decisions are an invisible failure mode. The owner had stated the project-root-boundary topology rules during ISOLATION-017 planning, but Prime Builder did not capture them as DELIBs. The owner had no way to know which of their statements had been considered for capture and rejected by AI judgment, vs which had been truly missed. This GOV closes that visibility gap.

Enforcement (cross-references):
- Will be registered as a cross-cutting requirement under GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 (when that GOV is created), enforced via the cross-cutting registry.
- The requirements-collection hook (per DELIB-S330-REQUIREMENTS-COLLECTION-HOOK-WITH-3-OPTION-CLARIFICATION) implements the positive-path surfacing for requirement_candidate classifications.
- The negative-path surfacing requires extension of the formal-artifact-approval gate hook OR a new PreToolUse hook that fires on AI's classifier outputs when classification == "chat" or "below_threshold".

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY. Source intent: I want to know when every specification is captured (or not captured) and I want to see the text of it when I approve or reject it.
change_reason: S330 spawn-queue item: GOV-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY-001 created from DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
